## Results

Descriptive joint group-by-wave growth for the historical cohort: each measure
keeps its own fitted group-by-wave cells, and the per-child stable offsets are
correlated across measures. These are **natural-history** estimates — `readgrp`
is a cohort factor, so between-group contrasts describe how the groups differ,
not what caused the difference, and the cross-measure correlations are
between-child associations, not within-child couplings.

### Cross-measure correlation of stable child levels (headline)

The posterior correlation matrix of the per-child stable offsets — do children
who sit persistently higher on one measure also sit persistently higher on the
others, net of their group's wave-by-wave trajectory?

_How to read the table:_ each row is one measure pair. `mean` is the posterior
correlation of the stable child levels (`lo`/`hi` its 95% credible interval)
and `prob_pos` the posterior probability the correlation is positive. The
matrix is shared across the three reading groups (a stated assumption).

```{python}
# | echo: false
corr = _csv("measure_correlation_summary.csv")
corr if corr is not None else "No correlation summary (run the fit first)."
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
mat = _csv("measure_correlation.csv", index_col=0)
mat if mat is not None else "No correlation matrix."
```

### Per-measure fitted cells and growth

One block per measure: posterior fitted group-by-wave cells against the
observed baseline (the audit view), and within-group interval growth on the
children observed at both endpoint waves. `window` marks complete-case **core**
cells vs the attrition-selected **extension** tail; extension cells carry their
own per-cell `n`.

```{python}
# | echo: false
_jc_extra = (config.get("extra") or {}) | (config.get("spec_extra") or {})
_jc_measures = list(_jc_extra.get("measures") or [])
_jc_labels = _jc_extra.get("measure_labels") or {}
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

for _m in _jc_measures:
    display(Markdown(f"#### {_jc_labels.get(_m, _m)} (`{_m}`)"))
    _cells = _csv(f"posterior_cell_summary_{_m}.csv")
    display(_cells if _cells is not None else Markdown("No cell summary."))
    _growth = _csv(f"posterior_growth_summary_{_m}.csv")
    display(_growth if _growth is not None else Markdown("No growth summary."))
```

- `posterior_population_mean` (+ `q2_5` / `q97_5`): expected item score for the
  group-by-wave cell on the population mean; `observed_complete_case_mean` is
  the observed mean it is calibrated against.
- Growth rows: `mean` (+ `q2_5` / `q97_5`) is posterior growth in items over
  the interval, computed on the `n_subjects` children observed at both
  endpoint waves; `p_gt_0` is the posterior probability it is positive.

::: {.callout-note title="No PSIS-LOO for this model"}

The model has one likelihood node per measure, so a single pointwise PSIS-LOO
is not defined for it; the posterior-predictive checks below are the fit
diagnostics. The per-measure `lrp-rlm-hg-*` models carry LOO for their own
single-measure fits.

:::
